channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people, but stopped short of acknowledging that Moscow was responsible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Drought, fires and deforestation battered Amazon rainforest in 2024
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — 2024 was a brutal year for the Amazon rainforest, with rampant wildfires and extreme drought ravaging large parts of a biome that’s a critical counterweight to climate change. A warming climate fed drought that in turn fed the worst year for fires since 2005.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US sanctions former Georgia prime minister amid political turmoil
item.description: The U.S. has imposed sanctions on Georgia’s former prime minister and billionaire Bidzina Ivanishvili, accusing him and the ruling party he founded of “undermining” the country’s “democratic and Euro-Atlantic future” to benefit Russia.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: IDF finds Hezbollah weapons cache in underground tunnel: video
item.description: The Israeli military said it discovered and destroyed an underground tunnel system used by Hezbollah to hide weapons used to attack Israel in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kazakhstan plane crash survivors say they heard bangs before aircraft went down, Putin issues statement
item.description: Survivors of the Azerbaijan Airlines plane that crashed in Kazakhstan on Christmas Day say they heard at least one loud bang before the aircraft crashed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Indian Prime Minister Manmohan Singh, who instituted economic reforms, cremated in New Delhi
item.description: Manmohan Singh, the former Indian prime minister widely regarded as the architect of the country’s economic reform program, has been cremated after a state funeral.
item.dc:creator: Aijaz Hussain, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli soldiers burn a hospital in Gaza
item.description: Israeli soldiers stormed and burned a hospital in isolated northern Gaza after forcibly removing staff and patients on Friday, Health Ministry officials said. The Israeli military said the hospital was being used by Hamas fighters as a base, although it did not provide evidence.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli airstrikes hit a Yemen airport as a jet with hundreds onboard was landing, UN official says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Israeli airstrikes hit Yemen’s main airport as a civilian Airbus 320 with hundreds of passengers on board was landing and a U.N. delegation was waiting to leave, the U.N.'s top humanitarian official in Yemen said Friday. Julien Harneis told U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NATO will step up Baltic Sea patrols as Finland probes the possible sabotage of undersea cables
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Friday that the military alliance will step up patrols in the Baltic Sea region as Finnish investigators work to establish whether a ship linked to Russia sabotaged undersea cables there this week.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Azerbaijan Airlines blames deadly plane crash on 'external interference' as Russia speculation grows
item.description: Azerbaijan Airlines has suspended flights to multiple Russian airports in the wake of a deadly plane crash, which it attributed to "physical and technical external interference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese man sentenced to death for killing 35 people after driving into a crowd
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A court in China has sentenced a man to death for killing 35 people last month by driving into a crowd, in an attack that raised national concern about mass killings.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: What to know about South Korean acting President Han's impeachment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The impeachment of South Korea's acting President Han Duck-soo Friday has plunged the country into further political turmoil, coming less than two weeks after lawmakers impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China unveils world's largest amphibious warship
item.description: China has launched a new amphibious assault ship, named the Sichuan, which is capable of launching fighter jets and is designed to compete with U.S. military strength in the seas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — The Chinese government placed sanctions on seven companies on Friday in response to recent U.S. announcements of military sales and aid to Taiwan, the self-governing island that China claims as part of its territory. The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Armed for survival: How Oct 7 Hamas massacre transformed gun culture in Israel
item.description: In the wake of the unprecedented October. 7 Hamas terrorist attacks, Israelis are having to grapple with the new reality of firearm ownership in a country where owning guns was once uncommon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Croatia's outspoken president is seeking reelection. He faces several contenders in Sunday's vote
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia’s left-leaning president, an outspoken critic of Western military support for Ukraine in its war against Russia, is running for reelection this weekend against an array of contenders, including the candidate backed by the conservative government.
item.dc:creator: SAŠA KAVIĆ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New European retail gig work app trend may influence US market
item.description: Stores such as Urban Outfitters, Lush and Uniqlo are recruiting gig workers in the U.K. to bulk up their stores.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: An uneasy calm settles over Syrian city of Homs after outbreak of sectarian violence
item.description: HOMS, Syria (AP) — Syria's new security forces checked IDs and searched cars in the central city of Homs on Thursday, a day after protests by members of the Alawite minority erupted in gunfire and stirred fears that the country's fragile peace could break down.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China probes personal disputes after mass killings. Many fear further infringement on freedoms
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The order came from the top. China's leader Xi Jinping wants the recent spree of mass killings that shocked the country not to happen again. He ordered local governments to prevent future “extreme cases.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's military intelligence says North Korean troops are suffering heavy battlefield losses
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — North Korean troops are suffering heavy losses in the fighting in Russia's Kursk region and facing logistical difficulties as a result of Ukrainian attacks, Ukraine’s military intelligence said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: WHO chief says he was at Yemen airport as Israeli bombs fell nearby
item.description: A wave of Israeli airstrikes hit Yemen's main airport Thursday just as the World Health Organization’s director-general said he was about to board a flight there. One of the U.N. plane’s crew was wounded, WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said in a post on X.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters that it was "necessary to wait for the end of this probe" before casting any speculation.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Finland seizes ship carrying Russian oil after undersea cable cut
item.description: Finland on Thursday seized a ship carrying oil for Russia in relation to the recent cutting of an undersea cable connecting electricity to Estonia as concern mounts over ships disrupting power and gas lines in European waters.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Moscow on Thursday warned against speculation that Russian air defenses may have caused the crash of an Azerbaijan Airlines plane in Kazakhstan this week that killed 38 people.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Biden condemns Russia's 'outrageous' Christmas attack on Ukraine
item.description: President Biden on Wednesday denounced Russia’s latest attack on Ukraine, declaring the wave of missiles and drones “outrageous.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: King Charles uses Christmas address to thank medical workers
item.description: King Charles III used his annual Christmas address to thank the medical workers who have provided care for the royal family and hailed the humanitarian workers “tirelessly” providing aid to those engulfed in conflict around the world.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: White House briefs Trump administration on nuclear threats
item.description: On Christmas Eve, tensions in the Holy Land persist as fears over nuclear weapon production in Iran and Pakistan amid the ongoing Israel war.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation
